LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S EX-PARTE APPLICATION FOR TRO & OSC RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION -- MS. HAYGOOD IS RESTORED TO HER POSITION AS DIRECTOR OF ELECTIONS EFFECTIVE IMMEDIATELY. ALL OTHER REQUESTS FOR TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER ARE DENIED. MR. GRIGGS TO PREPARE AN ORDER CONSISTENT WITH THIS RULING. THE COURT SET ON CALENDAR AN ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY A WRIT OF MANDATE SHOULD NOT ISSUE. THIS HEARING IS TO TAKE PLACE ON AUGUST 30, 2002 AT 11:00 A.M. BRIEFING SCHEDULE IS AS FOLLOWS: THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S WILL BE DUE ON AUGUST 15TH; ANY OPPOSITION BY THE CIVIL SERVICE COMMISSION OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND REAL PARTY IN INTEREST TAMMY B. HAYGOOD WILL BE DUE BY AUGUST 19TH; REPLY THERETO BY AUGUST 23RD. ALL SERVICE TO BE BY E-MAIL OR PERSONAL SERVICE BY 4:00 P.M. COURTESY COPIES TO BE PROVIDED TO THIS COURT. HON. JAMES J. MCBRIDE (301/MD); COURT REPORTER ERIC L. THRONE, CSR 7855

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Vernon C. Grigg III, DeMory Grigg (415) 956-7300, for Real Party In Interest Haygood
RULING:
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. Attorney Grigg represents to the Court that a settlement has been reached in this matter subject to approval by the Board of Supervisors; a stipulation to withdraw Motion For Restitution was filed 01-28-04. DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Sep-16-2002 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
* TAMMY b. HAYGOOD's demurrer to petition for Writ of Mandate is O F F C A L E N D A R -- Petition for writ of mandate denied on 9/3/2002. (JJM/301) Hon. JAMES J. McBRIDE, Court Reporter Eric Throne, CSR 7855

Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Sep-06-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Vernon C. Grigg, III, Esq. 957-7300 for Real Party In Interest TAMMY B. HAYGOOD applying for an alternative writ of mandate
Jerome B. Falk, Esq. and Steven L. Mayer, Esq. of HOWARD RICE 434-1600 for Petitioner and Plaintiff CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO opposing party
William Ferdinand Terheyden, Esq. of LITTLER MENDELSON 433-1940 for the CIVIL SERVICE COMMISSION OF THE CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
* A R G U E D and the Court granted the application for an alternative writ of mandate. However, the Court stayed this writ on the same terms as the Court of Appeal's stay on the Court's September 4, 2002 order. If the stay of the September 4th order is lifted, then the stay on this order is lifted. Mr. Grigg's request to restore Real Party In Interest TAMMY B. HAYGOOD to her requisition is granted. This order is also stayed. The July 29th Order (which is the Temporary Restraining Order) is still in effect. It is not dissolved because of the September 4th decision. This Order will proceed. Mr. Grigg's request for attorney fees is ordered off calendar. Mr. Grigg may renotice his motion after the Court of Appeal determines case. (JJM/301/md) Hon. JAMES J. McBRIDE Court Reporter Eric Throne, CSR 7855

Osc Why A Writ Of Mandate Should Not Issue
Off Calendar Aug-30-2002 Continued To Sep-06-02 At 12:00 P.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Vernon C. Grigg, III, Esq. 957-7300 for Real Party In Interest TAMMY B. HAYGOOD moving to strike extra record evidence. Also, applying for an alternative writ of mandate
William Ferdinand Terheyden, Esq. of LITTLER MENDELSON 433-1940 for the CIVIL SERVICE COMMISSION OF THE CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO joining in the motion to strike extra record evidence
Jerome B. Falk, Esq. and Steven L. Mayer, Esq. of HOWARD RICE 434-1600 for Petitioner and Plaintiff CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO opposing party
Peter Graham Cohn, Esq., M.S.W., J.D. 707/778-6945 for amicus curiae SAN FRANCISCO NAACP
* A R G U E D and the Court ruled as follows:
Motion to strike and judicial notice -- granted as to Exhibits 1 and 2 denied as to Exhibits 6, 9. Court will take judicial notice of ballot materials. Motion to strike as to Exhibits 36 thru 41 granted. Motion to strike Quinn Declaration with reference to separation papers granted. As to Real Party In Interest application for an alternative writ of mandate -- set that out for a status conference. Court will issue an order at the beginning of next week. Continued to Friday, September 6, 2002 at 9:30 a.m. (JJM/301/MD) Joyce Long, CSR 8620.

Plaintiff City & County Of San Francisco'S Ex-Parte Application For Tro & Osc Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jul-29-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jerome B. Falk, Jr., Esq. of HOWARD RICE 434-1600 for Plaintiff CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO applying for a temporary restraining order and an order to show cause re preliminary injunction
William Ferdinand Terheyden, Esq. of LITTLER MENDELSON 433-1940 for defendant CIVIL SERVICE COMMISSION OF THE CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO opposing party
Vernon C. Grigg III, Esq. of DE MORY/GRIGG 434-9800 for Real Party In Interest TAMMY B. HAYGOOD
* Ex-parte application argued. Recess at 1:10 p.m. Parties ordered back at 3:30 p.m. for a decision.
At 3:30 p.m. Court and counsel met in chambers. Back on the record at approximately 5:50 p.m.--the Court announced its decision as follows:
Ms. HAYGOOD is restored to her position as Director of Elections effective immediately.
All other requests for temporary restraining order are denied. Mr. Griggs to prepare an order consistent with this ruling.
The Court set on calendar an Order to Show Cause why a writ of mandate should not issue. This hearing is to take place on AUGUST 30, 2002 at 11:00 a.m.
Briefing schedule is as follows:
The CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's will be due on August 15th; any opposition by the CIVIL SERVICE COMMISSION OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO and Real Party In Interest TAMMY B. HAYGOOD will be due by August 19th; reply thereto by August 23rd.
All service to be by e-mail or personal service by 4:00 p.m. Courtesy copies to be provided to this Court. Hon. JAMES J. McBRIDE (301/md); Court Reporter ERIC L. THRONE, CSR 7855
